name: Cretan State (1898–1913)

colors.symbolism: the field of the Greek section

description: A blue field with a white cross extending to the edges, with a red canton in the upper hoist bearing a white five-pointed star.
history: Flag of the Cretan State, the autonomous polity established on Crete in 1898 under nominal Ottoman suzerainty after the Greco-Turkish War. The blue and white cross represented the Greek population; the red canton signified continued Ottoman suzerainty. The state was dissolved when Crete united with Greece in 1913 following the Balkan Wars.
